openEHR Description: A generic section header which should be renamed in a template to suit a specific clinical context.
EHDS Definition: Conditions and symptoms affecting the health of the patient.

openEHR Description: An overall statement of exclusion about all Problems/diagnoses Family history Medications Procedures Adverse reactions or other clinical items that are either not currently present or have not been present in the past.
Overall statement of exclusion of all problems or diagnoses at the time of recording.
openEHR Comment: For example: 'No previous diagnoses' 'No known family history' 'No known history of adverse reactions' 'Never taken any medications' or 'No known operations or significant procedures'.
EHDS Definition: Use if no conditions are listed

openEHR Description: Statement that specified health information is not available for inclusion in the health record or extract at the time of recording.
Positive statement that no information is available.
openEHR Comment: For example: No information available about adverse reactions; No information available about problems or diagnoses; No information available about previous procedures performed; or No information available about medications used.

openEHR Description: Description of the reason why there is no information available.
openEHR Comment: For example: patient is unconscious or refuses to provide information. Coding the reason with a terminology is desirable if possible.
EHDS Definition: Use if no conditions are listed

openEHR Description: Details about a single identified health condition injury disability or any other issue which impacts on the physical mental and/or social well-being of an individual.
openEHR Comment: Clear delineation between the scope of a problem versus a diagnosis is not easy to achieve in practice. For the purposes of clinical documentation with this archetype problem and diagnosis are regarded as a continuum with increasing levels of detail and supportive evidence usually providing weight towards the label of 'diagnosis'.
EHDS Definition: Model for a clinical condition problem diagnosis or other event situation or issue that has risen to a level of concern.

openEHR Description: Identification of the problem or diagnosis by name.
openEHR Comment: Coding of the name of the problem or diagnosis with a terminology is preferred where possible.
EHDS Definition: Code identifying the condition problem or diagnosis
EHDS Binding: ICD-10 SNOMED CT ICD-O Orphacode if rare disease is diagnosed

openEHR Description: A physical site on or within the human body.
EHDS Definition: EHDS refined base model for Body structure

openEHR Description: Identification of a single physical site either on or within the human body.
openEHR Comment:
This data element is the only mandated data point in this archetype and should be used as the primary data point to record an anatomical location with a commonly used name. It is strongly recommended that 'Body site name' be recorded as specifically as is anatomically possible. For example: record 'upper eyelid' rather than recording 'eyelid' with 'upper' as a qualifier; 'fifth rib' rather than 'rib' with a numeric qualifier. Use the other data elements for laterality aspect region and anatomical line to provide more detail.

This data element should be coded with a terminology capable of triggering decision support where possible - an appropriate termset for use here could comprise individual concepts or a list of precoordinated terms. Free text should be used only if there is no appropriate terminology available.

If 'Body site name' is already identified in an archetype in which this Anatomical location archetype has been nested then this data element may be redundant. However it might be reasonable to duplicate 'Body site name' in this archetype to support semantic querying using this archetype instead of the archetype in which it has been nested.
EHDS Definition: Body site

openEHR Description: Additional detail using a specific region or a point on or within the identified body site.
openEHR Comment:
Use to increase precision of identification of the body site if required. For example the upper right quadrant or McBurney's point on the abdominal wall or interphalangeal joint of the great toe. If the 'Body site name' data element uses pre-coordinated terms that include the specific site then this data element is redundant.
In most situations this will be a single specific site. This data element has multiple occurrences to support less common use cases which need to identify a single anatomical location involving two or more adjacent and continuous specific sites. For example to describe the specific site of a single tumour extending across the 2nd 3rd and 4th ribs.
EHDS Definition: Additional qualifier of the body structure (e.g. upper lower left side).

openEHR Description: The side of the body on which the identified body site is located.
openEHR Comment: If the 'Body site name' includes a pre-coordinated term that already specifies laterality this data element may be redundant. If the findings require documentation of a body site on both the left and right sides of the body or involving both sides of a paired structure or region use 'Left and right' only when the same anatomical site is affected on both sides and side-specific detail is not required. If the findings differ significantly between sides each should be recorded as a separate instance of this archetype with the appropriate laterality.
EHDS Definition: Body structure laterality (e.g. left right).
openEHR Values:
Left local:at0003
 Right local:at0004
 Left and right local:at0083

openEHR Description: Narrative description that can be used to further refine and support the 'Body site name'.
openEHR Comment: For example: adjacent to the vermilion border; a tattoo covers the bottom half of this area.
EHDS Definition: Textual description of the body structure

openEHR Description: Estimated or actual date/time that signs or symptoms of the problem/diagnosis were first observed.
openEHR Comment: Data captured/imported as Age at onset should be converted to a date using the subject's date of birth.
EHDS Definition: Onset date of a problem/condition

openEHR Description: An assessment of the overall severity of the problem or diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: If severity is included in the Problem/diagnosis name via precoordinated codes this data element becomes redundant. Note: more specific grading of severity can be recorded using the Specific details SLOT.
EHDS Definition: A subjective assessment of the severity of the condition as evaluated by the clinician.
openEHR Values:
Mild local:at0047
 Moderate local:at0048
 Severe local:at0049
-Other Text/CodedText allowed

openEHR Description: An overarching grouping for the problem or diagnosis identified in 'Problem/Diagnosis name'.
openEHR Comment: For example 'Neoplastic disease' 'Ischemic heart disease' or â€˜Anxiety disorderâ€™. Coding with an external terminology is preferred where possible.
EHDS Definition: Category or categories of the problem (e.g. POA - present on admission HAC - hospital aquired condition and other categorisations).
MappingNotes: The descriptions do not match exactly but are vague enough on both sides. Argument for making a Change Request to add a new ‘Diagnostic tag’ data element to the Problem/Diagnosis qualifier CLUSTER archetype?

openEHR Description: Details about findings that support a clinical assertion.
The result or finding that supports the assertion.
openEHR Comment: For example: '11 mm', '13 µg/L', '2.5 ml' or 'BRCA1 positive'. 'Result' can be coded with a terminology, where appropriate.
EHDS Definition: Stage/grade usually assessed formally using a specific staging/grading system. Multiple assessment systems could be used.
MappingNotes: We chose to align with the same pattern as the IPS template. However, the mapping between ‘Clinical evidence’ in openEHR (which can have e.g. a pathological stageing of a tumor, but also a reference to a blood pressure measurement) to stage has important limitations, depending on the use case

openEHR Description: Estimated or actual date/time of resolution or remission for this problem or diagnosis as determined by a healthcare professional.
openEHR Comment: Partial dates are acceptable. If the subject of care is under the age of one year then the complete date or a minimum of the month and year is necessary to enable accurate age calculations - for example if used to drive decision support. Data captured/imported as Age at time of resolution should be converted to a date using the subject's date of birth.
EHDS Definition: The date or estimated date that the condition resolved or went into remission

openEHR Description: Contextual or temporal qualifier for a specified problem or diagnosis.
Category that supports division of problems and diagnoses into Active or Inactive problem lists.
openEHR Comment: The Active/Inactive and Current/Past data elements have similar clinical impact but represent slightly different semantics. Both are actively used in different clinical settings but usually not together. If a Current/Past qualifier is recorded then this data element is likely to be redundant. An exception where a condition can be current but inactive is asthma that is not causing acute symptoms.
EHDS Definition: Status of the condition/problem (active resolved inactive ...)
MappingNotes: In version 0.3.0 of the EHDS LM, ‘problemStatus’ has been dropped

openEHR Description: Phase of healing for an acute problem or diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: For example: tracking the progress of resolution of a middle ear infection.
EHDS Definition: Status of the condition/problem (active resolved inactive ...)
MappingNotes: In version 0.3.0 of the EHDS LM, ‘problemStatus’ has been dropped

openEHR Description: Status of the remission of an incurable diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: For example: the status of a cancer or haematological diagnosis.
EHDS Definition: Status of the condition/problem (active resolved inactive ...)
MappingNotes: In version 0.3.0 of the EHDS LM, ‘problemStatus’ has been dropped

openEHR Description: Category of the occurrence for this problem or diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: This data element can be an additional qualifier to the 'New' value in the 'Episodicity' value set that is a condition such as asthma can have recurring new episodes that have periods of resolution in between. However it can be important to identify the first ever episode of asthma from all of the other episodes.
EHDS Definition: Status of the condition/problem (active resolved inactive ...)
MappingNotes: In version 0.3.0 of the EHDS LM, ‘problemStatus’ has been dropped

openEHR Description: Additional narrative about the Problem/Diagnosis qualifier values not captured in other fields.
EHDS Definition: This field includes free text if the resolution circumstances are not already included in other fields such as surgical procedure medical device etc. e.g. hepatic cystectomy (this will be the resolution circumstances for the problem hepatic cyst and will be included in surgical procedures).
MappingNotes: Argument for making a Change Request to add a new data element or to make ‘Comment’ multiple occurrence?

openEHR Description: The level of confidence in the identification of the diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: If an alternative valueset is required these values can be added to the DV_TEXT data type in a template.
EHDS Definition: Assertion about the certainty associated with a diagnosis. Diagnostic and/or clinical evidence of condition.
openEHR Values:
Suspected local:at0074
 Probable local:at0075
 Confirmed local:at0076
-Other Text/CodedText allowed

openEHR Description: External resource

EHDS Definition: External Resource which may be specifically related to the problem for example a link between a rare disease problem and the corresponding guidelines.
